I protect systems, just like a dragon protects its treasure.

★ The Highlight
The Iron Throne

A throne built from broken swords.

This throne was made by melting a thousand swords. It looks powerful, but it is not comfortable to sit on. That is the lesson I like most. Power is not a prize. It is a responsibility. In cybersecurity, I am not trying to sit on a throne. I am learning to protect one, one step at a time.

Aspiring Defender Most Promising Developer Steady, Pichai-style Growth Game of Thrones Devotee
I — About

Learning to defend what others build.

Dharshnaa Jagathisan

Hi, I am Dharshnaa Jagathisan. I am a Cybersecurity student. I love the discipline behind every safe system. It is the same discipline needed to hold a throne after winning it.

I am inspired by Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google. His calm way of leading and building things has shaped how I learn. I believe in steady progress, not shortcuts.

When I am not studying networks and weak points, I love watching Game of Thrones. It is a story about power, strategy and consequence. It is closer to cybersecurity than you might think.

II — Inspiration

Why I look up to Sundar Pichai.

Calm leadership, non-stop learning.

Sundar Pichai grew up in a small town in India. He became the leader of one of the biggest tech companies in the world. He stayed calm and curious through it all. That same mindset — quiet hard work over noise — is what I want to bring into cybersecurity.

"Focus on the user and all else will follow." — Sundar Pichai

Most Promising Developer.

Recognition

Awarded "Most Promising Developer"

This award reminds me I am on the right path. It proves that hard work and curiosity matter more than knowing everything already.

"Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts." — Motivation
Letter of Recognition

A note from my College Chairman.

This letter means a lot to me. It says my teachers see my interest in Apple app development, photography and filmmaking — and that hard work, even in first year, does not go unnoticed.
